reviews.review_msg:
Constantly talked about himself during lecture and 99% of the time it wasn't relevant (lowkey saviour complex). It's obvious there's no planning done in advanced because he reads the slides and makes something up. 
Exam wasn't challenging if you study using the textbook, lectures are useless. You don't receive any grades until the end of term.

Richard was a fun lecturer for this course, although at times it seemed like he would trail off on some tangents rather than lecturing on the slides. The textbook is your best friend for studying in this class.

Curt's lectures were very enjoyable with all the guest lecturers. Get ready to write very long papers for this course but they are take home! The questions for the papers are clear and the TA was approachable for re-grading. The class however did seem disorganized with the TA dropping out of the course and the professor not responding to emails.

Dr. Griffiths is a fantastic professor. He's super kind and very approachable. You can chat with him about course content, and he's more than happy to explain things and discuss his research. If you can take a course with him, DEFINITELY DO! In my opinion, he's one of the best Crim professors at SFU - do yourself a favour and take a course with him

I took policing in Canada with Curt. He was a nice prof but the course consisted of primarily presentations from different members of law enforcement so I did not get to see him teach. We had two take-home exams as well as a tutorial presentation, responding in tutorials, and general participation. Pretty chill and was interesting.

By far one of the greatest professors at SFU. He is the only professor I've met that will make an effort to go up to his students during breaks and ask them about their semester and interest in the class. Genuinely compassionate individual with a lot of knowledge. Brings in lots of guest lecturers which made the course even more amazing.

although the lectures are dry, most of the content is very straightforward and since this is the first time she's teaching the course, everything is very clear. the midterm was mostly from her lecture content so read the slides. there is a presentation in the tutorial
This class is a GPA killer and the exams are actually diabolical. This is NOT an easy elective.

Shannon is a very nice professor but the class itself is a lot of writing, there is weekly written portion, and attending lecture is mandatory because almost everything in her exams are from her lectures. TAKE GOOD NOTES. Final paper is 10-12 pages. Overall not bad but just a lot of work for what the class is.

Best criminology prof. If you go to all the lectures and write down everything she says, you will get a really good grade on the exams which are takehome exams and she gave us 3 days to do them. Amazing prof.

Best prof I've ever had. Super passionate and is very clear/well-spoken. Gives lots of good examples to help understand content. Advice - go to lecture and write down everything said. Exams are almost all lecture based, so if you go, take notes, and pay attention, it's easy to do well.

Good professor overall. There's a group project that's a presentation. Online homework is time-consuming. Tutorials are a must, and you have to participate if you want full marks. Does not post slides to canvas, and exams are lecture heavy, so lecture attendance is important. Term paper was easy to write (easy steps to follow). No textbook!

did not vibe with this prof at all, very old fashioned, and inspires very little. she is not open to students feedback, and her TA is just as useless as her, would never take this prof again despite earning a good mark.

One of the best lecturers at SFU. Clear slides and informative lectures, explains concepts so well you have no problem understanding the material. Exams are short-answer questions, but open-book, with all recordings of lectures available. Definitely recommend.

She is a solid prof. I have taken her a couple of times now and I found her to be fair and reasonable. One suggestion I have is to focus on what she talks about in lectures and the examples she gives.
She's overrated.
I don't like group projects but this was the one time where it was okay because we got to make our own groups. Great prof and hope she teaches more classes
Would take a course with Vienna again.
An angel. Take her class

i LOVE going to Viennas class. I am at sfu 8am-7pm and my last lecture is with her, i ALWAYS look forward to it. no matter how tired i am. I absolutely love her and wish i could take every one of her classes

Vienna is the most caring professor I have had at SFU. I had never taken a forensics class before, but she made sure the content was accessible and always encouraged questions. It's clear that she truly cares about her students' success and wellbeing. If you want a high grade in her class, she will support you in getting one.

Vienna is very nice and I admire her passion and dedication to the content and students. That being said, the assignment criteria was not very clear and resulted in some pretty poor marks (you have to read between the lines). The content is very scientific/medical which can be pretty difficult. Overall it was a good class, not my favorite though.
